name: Awkward Array (Ragged/Nested Array Operations)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-3); Scikit-HEP project; Jim Pivarski

performance.notes: C++ and GPU backends; vectorized operations on ragged arrays

agent_readiness.notes: NumPy-like operations on variable-length (ragged/nested) arrays. import awkward as ak. array = ak.Array([[1,2,3],[4,5],[6]]). ak.sum(array, axis=1). ak.flatten(array). From JSON: ak.from_iter(records). Parquet I/O: ak.from_parquet, ak.to_parquet. Arrow integration. GPU via CuPy backend. Originated in particle physics (HEP) for event data with variable-length track lists. Now widely used for any ragged/nested structure data. Integrates with uproot for ROOT file reading.

gotchas: Niche API — requires understanding of ragged array concepts
Performance benefits only for genuinely ragged data
best_when: Particle physics HEP data (variable-length tracks per event)
Ragged/nested JSON data with NumPy-style operations
NLP token arrays with variable sequence lengths
Graph neural networks with variable node/edge counts
avoid_when: Regular rectangular arrays (NumPy or pandas sufficient)
Simple JSON processing (standard Python dicts faster to understand)
Teams unfamiliar with NumPy broadcasting concepts
summary: Awkward Array provides NumPy-like operations on variable-length (ragged/nested) arrays. ak.Array([[1,2],[3,4,5]]) — vectorized sum, flatten, zip across unequal-length lists. Parquet/Arrow I/O, GPU via CuPy. Originated in particle physics for event data. Now used for NLP token arrays, graph data, any nested structure needing NumPy broadcasting semantics.
